place: Dansville village, New York, New York

facts.text: Dansville founded over 200 years ago and now hosts a renovated Main Street, modern library, and the county’s only hospital.
The Dansville Area Historical Society, created in 1961, preserves the town’s rich history through archives and public programs.
A historic fountain built in the 1880s sits in the middle of Main Street, a landmark damaged by a tractor‑trailer crash in 2016.
Dansville’s public library hosts a dedicated History of Dansville web page, offering digital resources for researchers and locals.
The town’s name honors Daniel Faulkner, an early settler who founded the village in 1795.
Dansville boasts several National Register of Historic Places landmarks, reflecting its unusually rich architectural heritage.
